person_name: Zeno of Citium (Zeno the Stoic, founder of Stoicism, c. 334–262 BCE)
person_name_full: Zeno of Citium (Zeno the Stoic, founder of Stoicism, c. 334–262 BCE)
notes: Founder of Stoicism, influential Hellenistic philosopher from Citium.

docs.title: BOOK XXXI
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: … in Bactria, 340 Zeno, a celebrated Stoic, 31 Zeugma, a town on the Euphrates, 179 Zianni, an Armenian tribe, 377 Ziata, a fortress in Mesopotamia, 193 Zinafer, a Sarmatian chief, 148 Zizais, son of a king of the Sarmatians, 148 Zombis, a town in Media, 337 Zopyrus, 169 Zoroaster, 336

docs.title: Contra Celsum, Book 5
docs.author: Origen
docs.excerpt: The disciples of Pythagoras, too, and of Plato , although they appear to hold the incorruptibility of the world, yet fall into similar errors . For as the planets, after certain definite cycles, assume the same positions, and hold the

docs.title: Letter 17
docs.author: Julian the Apostate
docs.subject_summary: There is indeed abundant evidence of other kinds that you have attained to the first rank in the art of medicine and that your morals, uprightness and temperate life are in harmony with your professio
docs.excerpt: … in the art of medicine, and that your moral character and temperate life match your professional skill. But now comes the crowning proof: though absent from Alexandria, you are winning the entire city to your cause. You have left a sting in her, like a bee's.
